title: Slice grid overlay geometry
acceptanceTarget: Canonical source-space cells render through explicit CSS and device-pixel transforms without drift across pan, zoom, ResizeObserver changes or DPR changes; the overlay never captures pointer input and cleans up without late writes.

inputs.transform: the existing Slice canvas viewport transform

mountSeamForG2_05.rule: Mount as an absolute sibling of the source canvas. Do not add an overlay store or recompute cells.

claims: all source, CSS and device rectangles remain inside source bounds
fractional zoom, negative pan and DPR 2.5 preserve exact shared edges
getters, sparse arrays, invalid numbers and OOB cells are rejected
late ResizeObserver callbacks cannot write after cleanup

focusedTypeBuild.result: 31 modules bundled; focused browser TypeScript seam compiled

browserVisual.visualInspection: passed: six cell outlines align with the non-divisible 7x5 colored fixture after negative pan, fractional 27.5x zoom, 420x260 resize and DPR 2

resourceCleanup.evidence: ResizeObserver/listeners are removed defensively, late callbacks are inert, backing canvas becomes 0x0, and no idle rAF loop exists.

accessibility.evidence: Overlay is aria-hidden and pointer-events:none on both host and canvas; Chrome pointer targeting reached the underlying stage.

repositoryTypecheck.reason: Task-local quality policy permits focused checks only while other agents mutate unrelated S1/Editor files.

independentReview.evidence: Independent 6/6 rerun and raw-diff review accepted canonical geometry, effective-layout compatibility, fractional transform/DPR edge preservation, hostile cleanup, pointer passthrough and fail-closed Chrome proof.

hostileAutopsy.strongestObjection: A standalone Canvas overlay could silently depend on Studio Tailwind utility classes and mis-size in a harness or future host.
hostileAutopsy.repair: Critical absolute/inset/overflow/display/pointer behavior is encoded inline as well as in Studio classes.
hostileAutopsy.closingProof: Chrome measured exact CSS/backing sizes before and after resize and DPR change; pointer passthrough and cleanup passed.
